human_verification.notes: Reviewed provider-network lifecycle boundary controls, roster/credentialing/contract/directory evidence expectations, PHI handling, clinical and payer escalation rules, research-source authority mapping, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required domain research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit for credentialing approval, contract approval, unsupported directory exceptions, payer interpretation, PHI disclosure, and licensed clinical judgment.
Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, and recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance.

Scenario map covers roster/credentialing/directory conflicts, onboarding and termination blockers, unsupported provider publication, PHI handling, eligibility/referral/authorization dependency, clinical and payer escalation, low-confidence routing, policy conflict, terminology handling, and completion evidence.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.
notes: The pack is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim trusted built authority.
Promotion to retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit build and approval event.
